title: Sanborn Fire Insurance Map from Texarkana, Bowie County, Texas, October 1885 initial sheet review manifest
origin_repository: Library of Congress Geography and Map Division

claim_boundary.verified_fact: This manifest records only labels and visible map features directly observed on the five locally validated LOC sheet derivatives.
claim_boundary.source_based_inference: Cross-sheet alignment, address normalization, business identity persistence, and any conversion into location or claim records remain deferred until later reviewed extraction.
claim_boundary.fictional_gameplay: This manifest creates no fictional gameplay records.

visible_features: Overview key and numbered sheet index in the upper left corner.
State Line and railroad corridors crossing the center of the sheet.
Large lumber yard and planning mill tract at the lower left.
Compass rose and scale bar shown on the unstitched sheet.
deferred_work: Translate numbered index blocks into stitched sheet coverage.
Extract stable business and street location records from the visible labels.
Reconcile rail corridor geometry with adjacent sheets before map alignment.
reviews.notes: This sheet combines the title cartouche, street and special index, legend, and a lower-panel industrial area; it is not a stitched town overview.

visible_features: Rail spurs, tramways, and mill frontage occupy the upper right quadrant.
Residential blocks 69, 70, 24, and 25 show scattered wells and frame buildings.
Broad, Spruce, Laurel, Hazel, Walnut, Clinton, and Oak labels organize the street grid.
Cross-sheet direction notes point toward sheet No. 3.
deferred_work: Confirm whether seminary and church labels should become stable location records.
Separate industrial rail frontage from nearby residential blocks.
Normalize cross-sheet references before any stitched-map work.
reviews.notes: The sheet mixes a mill district at the top and right with lower-density residential blocks and civic or institutional labels.

observed_labels: Texarkana Iron Wks. Foundry & Mach. Shop
visible_features: Dense multi-story commercial frontages line Broad and Front streets.
Public wells are marked inside the downtown blocks.
State Line Av. cuts diagonally across the lower portion of the sheet.
Cross-sheet notes connect this sheet to No. 4 and No. 5.
deferred_work: Review blocks 71 through 77 before promoting visible businesses into location records.
Distinguish hotel, livery, foundry, and postal uses in a later extraction pass.
Align the downtown frontage geometry with adjacent sheets before map reconstruction.
reviews.notes: This sheet is the most crowded commercial section in the initial review and contains many stacked occupancy labels that still need structured extraction.

visible_features: State Line Av. slices diagonally through the upper-right commercial blocks.
Multiple public wells are marked near the central business frontage.
Burned and ruins-of-fire annotations remain visible on several blocks.
Broad, State, Front, Maple, Spruce, and Clinton frame a dense downtown sheet.
deferred_work: Separate burned-site annotations from active businesses in a later pass.
Normalize the diagonal State Line frontage before geometry extraction.
Review candidate downtown landmarks before creating locations or claims.
reviews.notes: This sheet concentrates banks, hotel, opera-house frontage, and machinery-supply businesses without yet resolving parcel-by-parcel identities.

observed_labels: The Texarkana Cotton Seed Oil M'f'g Co.
The Arkansaw Cotton Seed Oil Mill.
visible_features: Multiple parallel railroad tracks and freight platforms dominate the left half of the sheet.
Cotton sheds, oil-mill buildings, compress structures, and cake houses fill the industrial tract.
A curving road and water edge appear on the east side of the sheet.
The Front Street hotel and depot cluster sits apart from the larger oil and compress complex.
deferred_work: Separate rail passenger, hotel, and industrial labels into later extraction passes.
Trace parcel boundaries through the compress and mill complex before georeferencing.
Avoid inferring ownership continuity across adjacent oil and compress structures.
reviews.notes: The sheet reads as a rail-and-industrial district centered on cotton seed oil and compress operations rather than a conventional street grid.
